# The Healing Play

> Play and creative arts therapy for children aged 4–14 in Ahmedabad, India, run by internationally certified play therapist Vishva Choksi (PTUK / PTI). Children work through emotions, behaviour, and difficult life moments through play — the language they know best.

The Healing Play is a solo private practice based in Bodakdev, Ahmedabad, Gujarat. It offers one-to-one child-centred play therapy and creative arts therapy, with particular experience supporting neurodivergent children (autism spectrum / ASD, ADHD, and special educational needs) and their parents.

## Who it helps
- Children aged 4 to 14.
- Concerns include big feelings and behaviour (meltdowns, anger), worry and anxiety, autism spectrum (ASD), attention and focus (ADHD), social and school struggles, and hard life moments (loss, a move, family change).

## Services
- Child-centred play therapy — one-to-one sessions for ages 4–14.
- Creative and expressive arts therapy — art, music, storytelling, and drama.
- Support for neurodivergent children — adapted for ASD, ADHD, and special educational needs.
- Parent and family counselling.
- Social-emotional learning groups — small-group sessions.

## The therapist
Vishva Choksi is a play and creative arts therapist certified through Play Therapy UK (PTUK) and Play Therapy International (PTI). Qualifications include a Post Graduate Diploma in Play & Creative Arts Therapy (PTUK/PTI), a Post Graduate Certificate in Therapeutic Play Skills (PTUK/PTI), an MA in Education (Creative Arts) from the University of Exeter (UK), a Post Graduate Diploma in Expressive Arts Therapy (East West Center of Counseling, Chennai), a BA in Psychology (St. Xavier's College, Gujarat University), and a Diploma in Child Psychology & Art Therapy.

## Practical details
- Location: Bodakdev, Ahmedabad, Gujarat, India.
- Hours: Monday to Saturday, 11 am to 7 pm. Closed Sunday.
- Contact: phone/WhatsApp +91 91577 42933, email thehealingplay@gmail.com.
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/thehealingplay/
- Booking: a consultation is booked via the contact form or WhatsApp; the first parent consultation carries no commitment.

## How it works
1. Say hello — enquiry form or WhatsApp, then a relaxed first conversation.
2. Parent consultation — Vishva meets the parents before any sessions begin.
3. Play sessions begin — usually once a week, at a rhythm that suits the child.
4. Regular reviews — progress is shared with parents at regular intervals.

## Note
Play therapy supports children's emotional wellbeing. It is not a substitute for medical, psychiatric, or psychological diagnosis or treatment.

## Key page
- Home: https://thehealingplay.com/
